regime: Risk-Off on Hot PCE (Gold Haven Bid Back, NQ Long Hangs On)
summary: Hot inflation flipped the tape risk-off and sent the haven bid back into gold. May core PCE printed +3.4% year over year, the highest since October 2023, with headline +4.1% and personal spending +0.7%, a firmer read that trims Fed-cut odds. Equities popped on the open then sold the rally all afternoon: MNQ closed 29,290 (-1.58%), hugging its 1hr trend stop just 30 points above the 29,260 line, while MES (-0.49%) flipped its stop long intraday but faded to close red and below 7,500. The split is the tell. Gold ran to fresh highs (+1.53%, ~4,104) as the inflation scare pulled the haven bid back, a mirror of last week's war-premium unwind, and crude fell -1.71%, flipping its 1hr stop short on growth worry. TSMOM-20 is technically still long but fragile into Jun 30 quarter-end. Trade the 29,260 line, not the story.

keyLevels.note: Long held by only 30 pts; the line

keyLevels.label: MES ATR Stop
keyLevels.note: Flipped long; 7,500 is the real confirm

keyLevels.label: MGC ATR Stop
keyLevels.note: Rising long; gold at fresh highs

setups.name: TSMOM-20 1hr (LONG, fragile)
setups.entry: Already long; the system holds long only while hourly closes hold above 29,260
setups.t1: AVWAP 29,472, then +2s 29,670 and the 29,717 RTH high
setups.bias: Defensive, not a fresh entry. The long sits 30 pts above its stop after a -1.58% day into quarter-end. No adds, no early exit; let the stop decide.

setups.name: ES Confirmation Watch (MES)
setups.entry: An hourly close above 7,500 confirms the equity complex long
setups.bias: MES flipped its stop long intraday but closed below 7,500; the alignment is on paper. Wait for the 7,500 hourly close before treating NQ/ES as aligned.

event.name: May PCE / Personal Income and Outlays (8:30 ET)
event.consensus: Core PCE expected near +3.3% to +3.4% y/y and +0.3% m/m; the question was whether inflation was re-accelerating into a still-firm consumer.
event.actual: Hot. Core PCE +0.3% m/m and +3.4% y/y, the highest since October 2023; headline PCE +4.1% y/y; personal spending +0.7%, topping forecasts. Firmer inflation plus firm spending trims near-term Fed-cut odds.
event.surprise: Inflation at or above the high end of expectations with the consumer still spending hard. Hawkish read.
event.nqReaction: MNQ popped on the print then sold the rally all session, fading from a 29,717 RTH high to close 29,290 (-1.58%) and holding its 1hr trend stop by only 30 points. The upside OR break (13:55 UTC) failed.
event.crossAsset: MES -0.49% to 7,398 (flipped its 1hr stop long intraday but closed below 7,500). MGC +1.53% to ~4,104, fresh highs on the haven and inflation-hedge bid. MCL -1.71% to 70.26, flipped its 1hr stop short on growth worry. Gold haven bid back; equities and crude weak.

observe.name: GAP_NQ (gap fade)
observe.note: No clean gap setup; the open pop was a PCE event move that reversed. Gap-fade stays suspect into quarter-end.

observe.name: TSMOM252LO_NQ (long-only 252)
observe.note: The longer-horizon trend is still long but the -1.58% day pulled price back toward the stop region. The 29,260 short-horizon stop is the early-warning line.

observe.name: ES confirmation
observe.note: MES flipped its 1hr stop long intraday but closed below 7,454 / 7,500. The alignment is on paper, not conviction; an hourly close above 7,500 is the real confirmation.

risk.throttleState: DEFENSIVE. TSMOM-20 is long by only 30 pts above its 29,260 stop after a hot PCE and a sell-the-rally session; ES has not confirmed above 7,500.
risk.ddProximity: Tight. The fragile 30-pt buffer means a single weak hour can stop the system out. Capital preservation over participation.
risk.dailyLossBudget: Intact, but the binding risk is realized chop, not a gap to the stop. No new long risk into quarter-end.
risk.sizing: Size small into the weekend. Respect the 29,260 stop; do not pre-empt by exiting early or defend by averaging down. Gold is extended beyond its +3s band and crude flipped short, but the long-only equity stack takes neither commodity trade.
risk.note: The binding constraint is forcing a trade in a thin, fragile, end-of-quarter market. Week ahead: Jun 30 quarter-end rebalancing, ISM Manufacturing Wed Jul 1, and the June jobs report Thu Jul 2 (pulled forward ahead of the Jul 3 Independence Day market close).

psychology: A stop is a line, not a hope. When price closes 30 points above your trend stop after a red day, the system is telling you the trend is on life support, and the disciplined response is to do nothing extra: do not exit early because the buffer feels thin, and do not add because you want the long to work. Both are emotional overrides of a mechanical edge. Today rewarded patience over prediction: the trader who shorted the open pop got squeezed, and the one who chased the relief rally got faded. Let Monday come to the 29,260 line and let the stop make the decision for you.

fullReport.title: Market Regime Snapshot (6-Axis)
fullReport.html:
AxisStateConfidence
DirectionRisk-off, haven bid to gold. MNQ -1.58% but TSMOM-20 long HELD its 29,260 stop by ~30 pts (fragile). MES flipped its stop long intraday then faded red. Gold strong (+1.53%, fresh highs); crude flipped short.MODERATE
VolatilityElevated. Post-PCE pop to 29,717 then a ~430-pt afternoon fade to 29,290. MNQ ATR(14) 196.HIGH
SessionEvent open (8:30 ET PCE). Upside OR break (13:55 UTC) that FAILED; faded back inside the OR, closed near RTH lows. Sell-the-rally.HIGH
InventoryLong / heavy from the post-PCE pop, distributed lower all afternoon. ON low 29,160; RTH faded -427 off the high.MODERATE
EventPOST_EVENT. May PCE hot: core +3.4% y/y (highest since Oct 2023), headline +4.1%, spending +0.7%. Next: Jun 30 quarter-end, Jul 1 ISM, Jul 2 jobs.HIGH
LiquidityThinning into Jun 30 quarter-end rebalancing and the July 4 holiday week.MODERATE

Cross-asset: hot PCE read hawkish. Equities sold the relief pop (MNQ -1.58%, MES -0.49%); gold caught a haven and inflation-hedge bid (+1.53% to ~4,104, fresh highs); crude fell (-1.71%, flipped short). This is the mirror of Jun 24's risk-premium unwind: the haven bid is back in gold, now on inflation rather than war.

fullReport.title: NY Opening Range / COMPASS (MNQ)
fullReport.html: NY Opening Range / COMPASS (MNQ). OR (13:30 to 13:44 UTC) 29,182 to 29,372.75, width 190.75. Break UP at 13:55 UTC (close 29,443 above the OR high) that FAILED: price could not hold above the OR, faded back inside and then below, closing 29,290 near the RTH low. An 8:30-data open made the first break suspect, and today the upside break was a trap.

fullReport.title: Momentum Breakout (Regime Filter)
fullReport.html: Momentum Breakout (Regime Filter). No clean long signal. The upside OR break failed and price made lower highs under AVWAP; the regime filter turned hostile to longs as price lost VWAP. RVOL was front-loaded into the open pop, not the trend, so there was no volume-confirmed breakout. No chase into quarter-end.

fullReport.title: AVWAP Deviation Band Reversal (MNQ)
fullReport.html: AVWAP Deviation Band Reversal (MNQ). Center 29,472, sigma 99.4. Bands: +1s 29,571, +2s 29,670, +3s 29,770; -1s 29,372, -2s 29,273, -3s 29,174. Price closed 29,290 near the -2s band, stretched to the downside but inside the band, with no qualifying volume-exhaustion reversal bar into the close. Re-qualifies only on a clean -2s/+2s tag with an exhaustion bar and a center reclaim, half size.

fullReport.title: Yesterday's Map: Marked (Jun 25 map, checked on the Jun 26 session)
fullReport.html:
#Map rowResultMeasured
1MNQ holds the re-armed 29,258 ATR stop and reclaims AVWAP 29,764 on hourly closes after PCETRIGGERED, MIXEDThe stop held (lowest hourly close 29,290, above 29,260) so the long stayed armed, but price never reclaimed 29,764; it rejected the 29,717 high and faded to close -1.58%. Held, did not repair.
2MNQ rejects AVWAP 29,764 and stalls below the +2s 29,918 into PCETRIGGERED, PLAYED OUTStayed well below 29,764 all day, then the reversion played out and overshot: price fell through NYVWAP 29,703 and the 29,500 psych to close 29,290.
3MNQ loses 29,500 and the 29,258 stop on a 2-close basis after PCENOT TRIGGEREDLost 29,500 on three afternoon closes, but the 29,258 stop held (lowest close 29,290). The compound condition never completed; the re-arm did not fail.
4MES reclaims and holds its 7,454 stop / 7,500 psych on an hourly closeNOT TRIGGEREDMES peaked at a 7,448 hourly close (15:00 UTC), just shy of 7,454, then faded to 7,398. Never reclaimed 7,454 / 7,500 on an hourly close; ES stayed the laggard.
5MGC holds its flipped 3,992 stop and MCL holds its flipped 70.63 stop on hourly closesTRIGGERED, MIXEDMGC held 3,992 and extended to 4,104 (+1.53%, continuation), but MCL lost 70.63 and closed 70.26 (-1.71%), flipping its 1hr stop short. Gold continuation, crude reversal.

fullReport.title: Tomorrow's Map (for Monday, June 29, 2026)
fullReport.html:
Map for Monday, June 29, 2026. Filters: Monday is a Mon-block, so BIR sits out; HOF, MID and BNC are eligible (the HOF 12:00 to 14:00 ET dead zone applies). No CPI or FOMC. Calendar: Monday Jun 29 has no first-tier US release (quiet start); Jun 30 is quarter-end rebalancing. The week builds to ISM Manufacturing on Wed Jul 1 and the June jobs report on Thu Jul 2 (pulled forward ahead of the Jul 3 Independence Day market close). All levels from today's 1hr chart.

yesterdaysMapScore.note: The hot-PCE sell-the-rally day. Row 2 (stay below 29,764, revert down) TRIGGERED and PLAYED OUT, overshooting through 29,703 and 29,500 to 29,290. Row 1 (hold 29,258 + reclaim 29,764) TRIGGERED and MIXED, the stop held but the reclaim failed. Row 5 (commodities hold flipped longs) TRIGGERED and MIXED, gold continued but crude broke and flipped short. Row 3 (2-close loss of 29,258) NOT TRIGGERED, the stop held by 30 pts. Row 4 (MES reclaims 7,454/7,500) NOT TRIGGERED, peaked at a 7,448 close.
